title: I'm a self-taught dev who landed $300K. Here's my exact path
post:
Eight years ago, I was working retail. Today, I cleared $300K last year as a senior engineer at a Series C startup.

My path wasn't efficient. I didn't go to a bootcamp. I didn't have a CS degree. I just... kept building.

I spent two years building side projects after work. Real ones — things people actually used. That portfolio got me my first job at a no-name startup for $55K. From there, I job-hopped every 18 months, doubling my comp each time.

The secret wasn't talent. It was compounding. Every project taught me something. Every job taught me what the market valued. I optimized for growth, not comfort.

If you're breaking in, know this: the degree doesn't matter after your first job. Your portfolio does. Your ability to learn does. Your willingness to take risks does.

It's not a straight line for anyone. But it is possible.

What's your career path been? Thoughts?
